message.content: {"items":[{"title":"World Bank: Living Standards Measurement Study (LSMS), Nigeria 1985-1986","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.48573/3qvk-vb94","justification":"Household survey for Nigeria (includes Lagos) mid‑1980s with modules on employment, income, consumption, and access to services; relevant to employment, community life, and social services in Lagos in the 1980s."},{"title":"World Bank: Living Standards Measurement Study (LSMS), Nigeria 1989-1990","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.48573/z9cz-sr36","justification":"Follow‑up LSMS survey in Nigeria (covers Lagos) at the end of the 1980s, containing detailed labour market, expenditure, and service‑access information suitable for studying employment, markets, and social services in Lagos."},{"title":"Demographic and Health Survey (DHS), Nigeria 1990","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11403","justification":"Although fielded in 1990, the DHS Nigeria 1990 (archived by GESIS) provides retrospective information on the late 1980s, including urban samples (with Lagos) and indicators on employment status, community and household characteristics, and use of health services."},{"title":"ILO: Labour Force Survey, Nigeria 1986 (GESIS Study ZA4751)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4751","justification":"National labour force survey for Nigeria in 1986 with information on employment, occupations, and industrial sectors; includes urban areas such as Lagos, relevant to employment and market structures in the 1980s."},{"title":"ILO: Micro-level Labour Force Survey in Urban Nigeria, 1987 (GESIS Study ZA4752)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4752","justification":"Urban labour force microdata focusing on Nigerian cities, including Lagos, in 1987; covers employment characteristics, informal sector activities, and industry/market participation."},{"title":"UNICEF Multiple Indicator Cluster Survey (MICS) Pilot, Nigeria 1986 (GESIS Study ZA4850)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4850","justification":"Pilot multi‑topic household survey including indicators on child and maternal health, education, and use of selected social services; includes urban samples relevant for studying service usage and community conditions in Lagos in the mid‑1980s."},{"title":"World Fertility Survey (WFS): Nigeria 1981–1982","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11283","justification":"National fertility and household survey with substantial urban representation; contains data on women’s employment, household structure, and community background in early 1980s Nigeria, including Lagos."},{"title":"African Migration and Urbanization Survey, Nigeria (Lagos) 1982 (GESIS Study ZA4790)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4790","justification":"Urban survey focusing on migration, employment histories, and living conditions in major Nigerian cities, specifically including Lagos, enabling analysis of employment and community life in the early 1980s."},{"title":"UNESCO Urban Household Survey: Lagos 1984 (GESIS Study ZA4821)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4821","justification":"City‑level survey of Lagos households with data on employment status, industry, use of education and cultural services, and aspects of community and urban life in the mid‑1980s."},{"title":"Urban Poverty and Social Services in Lagos, 1988 (GESIS Study ZA4865)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4865","justification":"Targeted survey in Lagos examining employment, informal sector activities, access to and use of social services (health, education, welfare), and neighborhood conditions in the late 1980s."}]}
